nodes.name: Nikola Tesla
nodes.description: Serbian-American inventor, electrical engineer, mechanical engineer, and futurist known for contributions to the modern alternating current electricity supply system and his contributions to the development of AC power.

nodes.name: Smiljan
nodes.description: Village where Nikola Tesla was born, part of the Austrian Empire (now Croatia).

nodes.name: Austrian Empire
nodes.description: Historical empire where Smiljan was located at the time of Tesla's birth.

nodes.name: Orthodox priest
nodes.description: Tesla's father's profession.

nodes.name: inventor of household appliances
nodes.description: Tesla's mother's profession.

nodes.name: Austrian Polytechnic
nodes.description: Institution where Tesla studied engineering.

nodes.name: Graz
nodes.description: City in Austria where the Austrian Polytechnic is located.

nodes.name: Charles-Ferdinand University
nodes.description: University in Prague that Tesla attended.

nodes.name: Prague
nodes.description: City in the Czech Republic where the Charles-Ferdinand University is located.

nodes.name: Budapest
nodes.description: City where Tesla worked at the Central Telephone Exchange.

nodes.name: Central Telephone Exchange
nodes.description: Place where Tesla conceived the idea for the induction motor.

nodes.name: Thomas Edison
nodes.description: Famous inventor and founder of the Edison Electric Light Company, for whom Tesla worked. Former employer and main rival of Tesla in the 'War of Currents'.

nodes.name: Edison Electric Light Company
nodes.description: Company established by Thomas Edison.

nodes.name: Tesla Electric Company
nodes.description: Company founded by Tesla in 1887.

nodes.name: AC induction motor
nodes.description: Device developed by Tesla that uses alternating current to create a rotating magnetic field.

nodes.name: Tesla coil
nodes.description: Device developed by Tesla that is essential for the AC power system. Device demonstrated by Tesla in 1891.

nodes.name: George Westinghouse
nodes.description: Founder of Westinghouse Electric Company who bought the patents to Tesla's AC system. Key business partner of Tesla who commercialized his AC patents.

nodes.name: Westinghouse Electric Company
nodes.description: Company founded by George Westinghouse.

nodes.name: War of Currents
nodes.description: Conflict between Edison (advocating DC power) and Tesla/Westinghouse (advocating AC power).

nodes.name: Edison
nodes.description: Advocate of DC power and competitor of Tesla and Westinghouse.

nodes.name: World's Columbian Exposition
nodes.description: Event in Chicago that chose AC power for its electrical system. Event for which Tesla won the contract to provide power.

nodes.name: Colorado Springs
nodes.description: Location where Tesla conducted experiments on wireless power transmission and moved for experiments in 1899.

nodes.name: Wardenclyffe Tower
nodes.description: Ambitious project by Tesla intended to transmit messages and power wirelessly across the Atlantic Ocean. Project funded by J.P. Morgan and associated with Tesla's work on wireless transmission.

nodes.name: J.P. Morgan
nodes.description: Main backer of Tesla's Wardenclyffe Tower project. Initial financial backer of the Wardenclyffe Tower project.

nodes.name: Shoreham, New York
nodes.description: Location where the Wardenclyffe Tower was constructed.

nodes.name: interplanetary messages
nodes.description: Signals Tesla believed he received from extraterrestrial intelligence.

nodes.name: New York City
nodes.description: City where Tesla spent his later years and died.

nodes.name: New Yorker Hotel
nodes.description: Hotel where Tesla died alone.

nodes.name: Edison's DC supporters
nodes.description: Group that conducted a campaign to discredit Tesla's AC system.

nodes.name: Guglielmo Marconi
nodes.description: Italian inventor who competed with Tesla over radio patents.

nodes.name: Mark Twain
nodes.description: Famous American writer and close friend of Tesla who visited his laboratory.

nodes.name: Swami Vivekananda
nodes.description: Indian philosopher who discussed science and religion with Tesla.

nodes.name: telautodynamics
nodes.description: System for transmitting mechanical power using radio waves, described by Tesla in a manifesto.

nodes.name: induction motor
nodes.description: Idea conceived by Tesla in Budapest.

edges.description: Nikola Tesla was born in the village of Smiljan.
Smiljan was part of the Austrian Empire at the time of Tesla's birth.
Nikola Tesla's father was an Orthodox priest.
Nikola Tesla's mother was an inventor of household appliances.
Nikola Tesla studied engineering at the Austrian Polytechnic in Graz.
Austrian Polytechnic is located in Graz.
Nikola Tesla attended the Charles-Ferdinand University in Prague.
Charles-Ferdinand University is located in Prague.
Nikola Tesla moved to Budapest in 1882.
Nikola Tesla worked at the Central Telephone Exchange in Budapest.
Nikola Tesla conceived the idea for the AC induction motor while working at the Central Telephone Exchange.
Nikola Tesla went to work for Thomas Edison in 1884.
Thomas Edison was establishing the Edison Electric Light Company at the time Tesla worked for him.
Nikola Tesla worked for the Edison Electric Light Company.
The War of Currents was a conflict between Tesla and Edison.
Nikola Tesla founded the Tesla Electric Company in 1887.
Nikola Tesla developed the AC induction motor.
Nikola Tesla developed the Tesla coil.
George Westinghouse founded the Westinghouse Electric Company.
George Westinghouse bought the patents to Tesla's AC system.
Edison advocated for DC power during the War of Currents.
Westinghouse bought the patents to Tesla's AC system.
Westinghouse is the founder of Westinghouse Electric Company.
Westinghouse was involved in the 'War of Currents' against Edison.
Edison opposed Tesla's AC power.
Tesla advocated for AC power alongside Westinghouse.
Westinghouse Electric Company won the contract to generate electricity for the World's Columbian Exposition.
Tesla moved to Colorado Springs to conduct experiments.
Tesla believed he received signals from extraterrestrial intelligence, which he called 'interplanetary messages'.
Tesla began construction of the Wardenclyffe Tower.
Tesla's Wardenclyffe Tower project was backed by J.P. Morgan.
J.P. Morgan withdrew support for the Wardenclyffe Tower project.
Wardenclyffe Tower was located in Shoreham, New York.
Wardenclyffe Tower was an ambitious project of Nikola Tesla.
Spent his later years living in hotels in New York City.
Died alone in Room 3327 of the New Yorker Hotel.
Wrote a manifesto about telautodynamics, describing a system for transmitting mechanical power using radio waves.
Former employer and main rival in the 'War of Currents'.
Conducted a campaign to discredit Tesla's AC system.
Competed with Tesla over radio patents.
Key business partner who commercialized his AC patents.
Initial financial backer of Wardenclyffe Tower project.
Close friend who visited Tesla's laboratory.
Discussed science and religion with Tesla.
Conceived the idea of the induction motor.
Demonstrated the Tesla coil.
Won the contract to power the World's Columbian Exposition.
Died in New York City.
